evidenceSnippetOriginal: Using somebody else's work without crediting the source – including generative AI — is plagiarism. Guided by the policies of faculty, instructional teams, and staff, AI-generated work should be cited like any other reference material, including how and where students used AI-generated information.
evidenceLocator: Wharton Academy > Statement of AI Use section
